message:
refactor(cass): improve argument ordering for CLI command safety

Refactor cass command building to use proper argument ordering pattern:
options before -- separator, then positional arguments after.

Changes:
- cassSearch(): Extract duplicated arg building into buildCassSearchArgs
- buildCassSearchArgs(): Place query after '--' for shell safety
- cassExport(): Reorder args so format comes before '--' and path after
- cassExpand(): Same pattern - options first, then '--', then session path

This follows POSIX convention where '--' marks end of options, preventing
query/path strings from being interpreted as flags if they start with '-'.

Example before: ["search", "query", "--robot", "--limit", "5"]
Example after:  ["search", "--robot", "--limit", "5", "--", "query"]

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -37,3 +37,9 @@ beads.right.meta.json
 # contributors to accidentally commit upstream issue databases.
 # The JSONL files (issues.jsonl, interactions.jsonl) and config files
 # are tracked by git by default since no pattern above ignores them.
+
+# Local history backups
+.br_history/
+
+# bv (beads viewer) lock file
+.bv.lock
